## Understand the architecture before rewriting the UI

NA-AI Landing is organized around a simple separation:

> Data controls content. Sections render product blocks. Layout components frame the page. PyColors UI provides primitives.

This keeps customization clean, predictable, and easier to maintain as the landing page evolves.

Most product content can be updated from:

```txt
src/@data
```

before touching the visual implementation.

<DocsUpgradeCTA
  title="The structure is optimized for fast iteration."
  description="NA-AI Landing separates content, sections, layout, and UI primitives so you can customize positioning and branding without rebuilding the foundation."
  productSlug="na-ai-landing"

<Callout title="Key idea">
  The template is designed so product content can evolve without constantly rewriting the visual foundation.
</Callout>

## Structure

<Files>
  <Folder name="src" defaultOpen>
    <Folder name="@data" defaultOpen>
      <File name="contact.ts" />
      <File name="cta.ts" />
      <File name="customers.ts" />
      <File name="faq.ts" />
      <File name="features.ts" />

    <Folder name="app" defaultOpen>
      <File name="globals.css" />

      <File name="page.tsx" />
      <File name="not-found.tsx" />

    <Folder name="components" defaultOpen>

      <Folder name="sections">
        <Folder name="features" />
        <Folder name="hero" />
        <File name="contact.tsx" />
        <File name="cta.tsx" />
        <File name="customers.tsx" />
        <File name="faq.tsx" />
        <File name="integrations.tsx" />
        <File name="pricing-table.tsx" />
        <File name="pricing.tsx" />
      </Folder>

      <Folder name="ui">
        <File name="browser-window.tsx" />
      </Folder>
    </Folder>

    <Folder name="config">
      <File name="fonts.ts" />
      <File name="site.ts" />
    </Folder>
  </Folder>

  <Folder name="public" defaultOpen>
    <Folder name="icons" />
    <Folder name="na-ai" />
    <Folder name="seo" />
    <File name="manifest.webmanifest" />
  </Folder>
</Files>

### Architecture flow

```txt title="architecture-flow.txt"
src/@data
  ↓
components/sections
  ↓
components/layout
  ↓
@pycolors/ui + @pycolors/tokens
```

## Data directory

| File | Responsibility |
| --- | --- |
| `hero.ts` | Hero copy, CTA, badges, and visual content |
| `features.ts` | AI analytics feature cards and section content |
| `integrations.ts` | Integration categories and integration cards |
| `pricing.ts` | Pricing plans and billing toggle content |
| `pricing-table.ts` | Pricing comparison rows |
| `customers.ts` | Testimonials and customer proof |
| `faq.ts` | FAQ content |
| `contact.ts` | Contact form copy and goal options |
| `cta.ts` | Final conversion section |
| `navbar.ts` | Navigation links |
| `footer.ts` | Footer links and footer copy |

<Callout title="Customization rule">
  Change data first. Edit section components only when the current structure no longer supports the product story.
</Callout>

## Add a new section

<Steps>
  <Step>
    ### Create the data file

```bash title="terminal"
touch src/@data/use-cases.ts
```
  </Step>

  <Step>
    ### Create the section component

```bash title="terminal"
touch src/components/sections/use-cases.tsx
```
  </Step>

  <Step>
    ### Compose the section inside the page

```tsx title="src/app/page.tsx"
import { UseCasesSection } from "@/components/sections/use-cases";

export default function Page() {
  return <UseCasesSection />;
}
```
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Recommended architecture rules

<PreferAvoid
  prefer={[
    'data-driven content',
    'small section components',
    'shared PyColors primitives',
    'semantic tokens',
    'isolated template-specific UI',
  ]}
  avoid={[
    'hardcoding all copy in components',
    'putting backend logic in sections',
    'duplicating layout wrappers',
    'removing token imports',
    'forking shared UI primitives unnecessarily',
  ]}
/>

### Why this structure scales

As landing pages grow, problems usually come from unclear boundaries:

- copy mixed with layout logic
- duplicated sections
- inconsistent spacing
- scattered pricing values
- disconnected SEO assets
- uncontrolled visual overrides

This structure reduces those problems by keeping responsibilities separate from the beginning.

<Callout title="Scaling principle">
  A good landing page architecture should make iteration cheaper over time, not more fragile.
</Callout>

## Common questions

<Accordions type="single">
  <Accordion title="Why keep content in src/@data?">
    It keeps section components focused on presentation and makes the landing page easier to customize quickly.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Can I move data elsewhere?">
    Yes. Keep the same separation: content should stay independent from presentational components.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Can I connect a CMS?">
    Yes. Replace the local data source gradually while keeping section components stable.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Can I remove PyColors UI?">
    You can, but it is usually not recommended unless you want to rebuild the UI foundation yourself.
  </Accordion>
</Accordions>
